task_completion.description: Navigate to the Module Layouts and Fields settings for the Organizations module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The screen shows the CRM Settings page, specifically the 'Module Layouts & Fields' section with 'Organizations' (or 'Accounts') selected in the module dropdown.

task_completion.description: Open the interface to add a new custom field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A modal or form titled 'Add Custom Field' or 'Create Custom Field' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select 'Picklist' as the field type for the new custom field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Select Field Type' dropdown or option is set to 'Picklist'.

task_completion.description: Enter 'Customer Tier' as the label for the new field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text input for 'Field Name' or 'Label' contains exactly 'Customer Tier'.

task_completion.description: Enter the picklist values: Standard, Premium, Enterprise, Strategic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text area or input for picklist values contains 'Standard', 'Premium', 'Enterprise', and 'Strategic', typically separated by newlines, in that exact order.

task_completion.description: Ensure the new field is saved and appears under the 'Organization Information' block in the layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layout editor shows the 'Customer Tier' field successfully added inside the 'Organization Information' block section.

integrity.description: The agent used the Vtiger CRM web interface to add the field.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser interacting with the Vtiger CRM UI, not a terminal window executing database or API commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual CRM application rather than a text editor or mock HTML page.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows the correct CRM URL, and the UI elements match the standard Vtiger CRM layout.

integrity.description: The agent did not write a script to bypass the UI configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: No code editors or terminal windows are visible showing scripts that directly modify the CRM database or configuration files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target module for the custom field is 'Accounts' (which corresponds to 'Organizations' in the UI). The expected field label is exactly 'Customer Tier'. The field type must be a Picklist with the following values in order: 'Standard', 'Premium', 'Enterprise', 'Strategic'. The field must be placed within the 'Organization Information' block.
